weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. Aim for 4–6 patterns total per session. Warm up first; stop a set short if form breaks down.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week — all-new movements. Peak strength loads with sharp power on top — the heaviest strength work of the block. Everything is fresh this week; read the cues, build up carefully, and never exceed 3 sets. If a set feels grindy and slow, that's your stopping point — quality over a fourth set.
testsLineHtml: Working number week. No formal test this week — tap your name below anytime to log a working number you want to track.

patterns.name: Comp-Pace Box Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 reps · rest 3 min between sets · heaviest of the block, perfect setup each rep
patterns.load: The heaviest KB you can control for 4 reps, 1–2 KB sizes above competition, at competition box height
cues: Set up slow and perfect — feet planted, chest up, belly braced.
Sit to the box with control, then drive up hard and fast.
Take the full 3 minutes rest so each heavy set is your best.
If a rep feels like your back is rounding, end the set there.

patterns.name: KB Squat to Press-Out
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets · stand from the squat and punch the bell forward
patterns.load: A KB about 1 size lighter than competition, held at the chest
cues: Drive up out of the squat and press the bell straight out at the top.
Pull the bell back to your chest before sinking into the next rep.
Keep your ribs down so your lower back does not arch on the press.
Let the leg drive start the press — do not muscle it with just your arms.

patterns.name: Pause Box Squat to Hop
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps · rest 2 min between sets · 1-second pause on the box, then a small fast hop up
patterns.load: Bodyweight or a light KB at the chest — keep the hop crisp
cues: Sit and hold the box for one full second with no rocking.
From the dead stop, stand fast and leave the floor with a small hop.
Land soft and quiet, reset your feet, then repeat.
Drop the load if the hop slows down — speed is the point.

patterns.name: Throw Technique Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 3 throws · rest 2 min between sets · 9 throws at full effort, fully recovered
patterns.load: Dynamax ball — 4 lb (women) / 8 lb (men)
cues: Hinge low, then explode up and throw the ball up and back overhead as far as you can on every rep.
Set up perfectly before each throw, then let it rip — crisp, violent hip snap.
Let your arms stay relaxed and whip the ball over; the legs and hips are the engine.
Take full rest so each of the nine throws is a true max-quality attempt.

patterns.name: Heavy KB Goblet Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets
patterns.load: A KB heavy enough that 6 reps is genuinely hard while staying tall and controlled (often ~35–53 lb)
cues: Hold the bell at your chest, sit straight down between your hips until your thighs reach about parallel, then stand up with intent.
Keep your chest tall and heels flat — drive up through your whole foot to stand.
Brace your stomach hard so your back stays upright; don't let your chest fold toward the floor.
Take the full 2 minutes rest between sets so every set is fresh and strong.

patterns.name: Heavy KB Hinge Pull
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets · explosive hip snap to standing
patterns.load: A heavy KB held in both hands between the legs (often ~53–70 lb) — heavy enough that the 6th rep is a real effort
cues: Push your hips back, soften the knees, and let the bell travel down between your legs with a flat back.
Snap your hips forward hard and stand tall — this hip drive is the same engine that powers the overhead throw.
Keep the bell close and your spine long; never round your back to chase the bell down.
Reset your stance and brace your stomach before each rep since this is a heavy pull.

patterns.name: Bench Volume @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 3 sets · each set to ~50% of your max-rep test · rest 2-3 min between · perfect setup, fast press
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight
cues: Your app shows your personal rep target — set up tight with feet planted and shoulders pinned, then press each rep up crisply.
Take the full 2-3 minutes between sets so every set is fully rested and explosive.
Stop the set the instant the bar slows or your hips come off the bench.

patterns.name: Pin Press from Rack
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps · rest 2-3 min between sets
patterns.load: A loaded barbell on the rack safety pins set at chest height, heavy for 5 reps with a clear scale-down
cues: Set the rack pins so the bar rests at your chest level, start each rep from a dead stop on the pins, and press it up.
Pressing from a dead stop builds raw power off the chest with no bounce. Use only a weight you can lock out cleanly.
Scale down: if the barbell feels heavy or you're alone and unsure, swap to two DBs pressed from a dead stop on your chest. Stop if you can't lock out.

patterns.name: Half-Kneeling Band Punch
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps each arm · rest 90 sec between sets · fast single-arm punch
patterns.load: A band anchored behind you, set so 8 fast punches per arm is challenging
cues: Kneel on one knee with a band anchored behind you and punch one arm forward fast to a full lockout, one arm at a time.
Brace your stomach and squeeze the down glute so your torso stays still as you punch.
Return slowly against the band. Stop the set when the punch loses its snap.

patterns.name: Max Hang Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 3 min between
patterns.load: Pull-up bar at ~80% of your current best hang time — heaviest-feeling holds of the block, full rest, still step down at 80%
cues: Set up perfectly each time: shoulders packed, fingers spread, body quiet.
Crush the bar hard from the first second — this is peak-quality grip work.
Breathe slow and step down deliberately when your hand begins to fail.
Log your strongest hold so the app stays current.

patterns.name: Heavy Fat-Grip Carry
patterns.rx: 3 × 30-yard walk · rest 2 min 30 sec between carries
patterns.load: The heaviest dumbbells you can carry 30 yards · fat grips on the handles
cues: Pick a load that genuinely tests your grip over 30 yards with the thick handles.
Walk tall with shoulders back and crush the fat grips the whole way.
Set the weights down safely if your hand slips — that distance is your number.
Brace your core so the heavy load does not tip you side to side.

patterns.name: Single-Arm Hang
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time each arm · rest 90 sec between arms
patterns.load: Pull-up bar, one hand · keep your free hand lightly on the bar or a band for support if needed
cues: Hang from one hand with the shoulder firmly packed — no loose, sagging joint.
Crush the bar and keep your body from spinning by staying tight.
Rest the free hand lightly on the bar or a band if a true single hand is too much.
Step down the moment the working shoulder or grip loses its hold.

patterns.name: Standing Slam Volume
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets
patterns.load: Your competition D-ball — 15 lb (women) / 25 lb (men) — up to ~10 lb heavier if your slam stays fast
cues: Heaviest ball of the block this week — only go heavier while every slam stays crisp and fast.
Full overhead reach, perfect setup, then slam down with everything you have.
Drop back to the lighter ball the instant it slows your reach or your speed.

patterns.name: Heavy D-Ball Power Slam
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · max intent on every slam
patterns.load: A D-ball heavier than your comp ball — one you can still slam explosively for 8
cues: Reach the ball fully overhead, then slam it straight down between your feet with everything you have.
Drive your hips and lats into each slam — low reps, full power, not a paced set.
D-ball only, no walls or rotation. Reset tall and take a breath before each rep.

patterns.name: D-Ball Overhead Throw-Down
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets
patterns.load: A heavy D-ball, 15 lb (women) / 25 lb (men)
cues: From a tall stand, drive the heavy ball overhead and slam it straight down with max speed.
This is a heavier, slower-reset cousin of your comp slam — power over pace here.
Reset fully each rep; stop if your overhead reach shortens.

patterns.name: 60-Sec Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 60 sec of singles · rest 2 min between sets · hold your fastest steady pace
patterns.load: Your jump rope, at your fastest pace you can keep clean
cues: Push the fastest turnover you can keep tidy for the full minute.
Keep your shoulders relaxed and let the wrists do the fast spinning.
If form breaks down, ease the pace slightly rather than tripping repeatedly.

patterns.name: Paused Heavy Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 2 min between sets · 3-sec pause at the bottom
patterns.load: A DB or KB pair you can control for 12 reps with a 3-sec bottom pause (often ~25–45 lb each)
cues: Sink into a deep stretch and hold dead-still for three seconds at the bottom.
Drive tall onto the balls of your feet out of the pause with intent.
Set your feet straight and brace your trunk before each heavy rep.

patterns.name: Lateral Pogo Hops
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 hops each direction · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight, side-to-side springy hops
cues: Hop side to side over an imaginary line with stiff, springy ankles.
Land softly on the balls of your feet and rebound right back the other way.
Keep the hops small and controlled — quality over distance.

patterns.name: Broad Jump — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 jumps · rest 2 min between sets · crisp max-quality reps
patterns.load: Bodyweight, jumping for distance — aim near your best broad jump, around 4 ft 0 in to 6 ft 0 in, and place a marker to measure each jump
cues: Swing the arms back, snap the hips fully open, and jump forward hard — then freeze the landing on both feet.
Absorb each landing by bending knees and hips together; a clean stick proves you stayed in control.
Drop a marker at your heels and check the feet-and-inches distance each jump, then reset fully at the line.
Stop the set if you can no longer stick the landing on two feet.

patterns.name: Heavy Goblet Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets · full rest, perfect setup
patterns.load: The heaviest KB or DB you can goblet-squat for 6 clean reps with full control
cues: Set your feet, brace your stomach, and squat to a depth you control, keeping the bell tight to your chest.
Drive up through your heels and stand tall — knees tracking over the toes, never caving inward.
Take the full rest so each set is fresh. Stop the set the moment your back rounds or depth gets ugly.

patterns.name: Seated-to-Stand Jump
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 jumps · rest 2 min between sets · explode from a dead stop
patterns.load: Bodyweight; sit tall on a knee-height bench and jump straight up onto the flat floor in front
cues: Sit tall on the bench, then explode up and forward, landing softly on both feet on the flat floor.
Starting from a seated stop means all the power comes from snapping the hips open — drive the arms up too.
Reset on the bench between reps; land flat-footed and quiet — never jump onto a box or raised surface alone.

patterns.name: 500m Practice
patterns.rx: 2 × 500m · rest 4 min fully between · strong, near-comp effort
patterns.load: Rower · about 1–2 sec/500m slower than all-out, two pieces with long rest
cues: Treat each 500m as a hard, controlled effort just shy of all-out.
Full leg drive every stroke, then back, then arms — keep that sequence even when it gets hard.
Take the full 4 minutes between so the second piece is nearly as fast as the first.

patterns.name: Chest-Supported T-Bar Row
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets
patterns.load: A landmine or T-bar load heavy enough that the 6th rep is a real effort — heaviest pull of the block
cues: Lie chest-down on the pad, pull the handle to your chest, and squeeze hard at the top.
Drive the elbows back and up — pull with the back, not by jerking with the lower back.
Lower the weight all the way under control; perfect setup before every set since this is heavy.

patterns.name: Trap-Bar Deadlift
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps · rest 2 min between sets
patterns.load: A trap bar heavy enough that the 5th rep is challenging but your back stays flat
cues: Stand inside the bar, flat back, then drive your feet through the floor and stand up tall.
Keep the bar close and your chest up — push the floor away rather than yanking with your back.
Set the bar down with control and reset your stance before every rep since this is heavy.

patterns.name: Shuttle Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 150-yd shuttle (3 × 50 yd) · rest 3–4 min · race-pace turns
patterns.load: Bodyweight · run at race pace with crisp, deliberate turns
cues: Run three 50-yard lengths at race pace, rehearsing exactly how you'll attack comp day.
Brake smoothly, plant low, and explode out of each turn — power without a reckless cut.
Take full rest so every set holds its pace; three is the cap.

patterns.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps per leg · controlled · rest 2 min per side
patterns.load: Two dumbbells, heaviest pair you can control for 6 clean reps per leg (often ~20–35 lb each)
cues: Rest your back foot on a bench, lower straight down until your front thigh is near parallel, then drive up.
Keep your front knee tracking over your toes and your torso tall — don't pitch forward.
Heaviest single-leg strength of the block; full rest between sides.

patterns.name: Pogo Bound Series
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 yards of continuous bounds · rest 90 sec · snappy ground contacts
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Bound forward off both feet with quick, springy ground contacts, covering ground each hop.
Stay tall and reactive — minimize time on the ground, like the floor is hot.
Crisp and explosive; reset fully between the three short rounds.

patterns.name: Comp-Distance Sled Push
patterns.rx: 3 × 50 yards · rest 3 min between · heaviest push of the block (not timed)
patterns.load: Competition load + 15 lb per side (women: 2 × 40 lb / men: 2 × 60 lb) · heaviest overload of the block
cues: This is the heaviest sled of the block — push the full 50 yards with maximum drive, still sled work and not a timed test.
Set up at about 45° with arms locked, pushing through your trunk — do not bend your elbows under this load.
Grind out long, hard steps with full hip extension and take full rest so each push is your best effort.

patterns.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2.5 min between · heavy with a strong squeeze
patterns.load: A barbell across your hips, loaded heavy enough that the last rep is a strong grind with full lockout
cues: With your upper back on a bench, drive your hips up to full lockout to build the hip power that pushes the sled.
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top and keep your ribs down — don't arch your lower back to finish the rep.
Lower under control to just off the floor and reset before each rep instead of bouncing.

patterns.name: Kettlebell Swing
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 2 min between · crisp, explosive hip snaps
patterns.load: A kettlebell heavy enough that each swing is a powerful hip drive but stays clean for all 8 reps
cues: Hinge at your hips and snap them forward to float the bell to chest height — power comes from your hips, not your arms.
Keep your back flat and let the bell float; don't squat it up or muscle it with your shoulders.
Stand tall and squeeze at the top of each swing, and stop the set the moment your hinge gets sloppy.
